name: mypy-boto3-builder / boto3-stubs (AWS Type Annotations)

auth.notes: Type stubs only; auth is boto3's responsibility

performance.notes: Type annotations only; zero runtime overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Type stubs for boto3 enabling IDE completion and mypy checking. pip install 'boto3-stubs[s3,dynamodb,sqs]'. import boto3; from mypy_boto3_s3 import S3Client. client: S3Client = boto3.client('s3'). client.get_object(Bucket='b', Key='k') → GetObjectOutputTypeDef with full type info. All 300+ AWS services have stubs. Service-specific stubs: boto3-stubs[ec2], boto3-stubs[lambda], etc. paginator: S3Client.get_paginator('list_objects_v2') → typed paginator. Type: GetObjectOutputTypeDef, PutObjectRequestRequestTypeDef. IDE autocomplete for all boto3 API params. mypy catches wrong param types. Full coverage of all AWS APIs matching current boto3 version. No runtime behavior change — pure type annotations.

gotchas: Install only the service stubs you need — full install is very large
Must keep boto3-stubs version in sync with boto3 version
Some IDE integrations require explicit type annotation (not just client = boto3.client())
TypedDict return types can be verbose in type errors
best_when: Any boto3 codebase wanting IDE autocomplete and type checking
Teams using mypy or pyright for static analysis
Reducing boto3 API errors caught at runtime (catches at type-check time)
avoid_when: Quick scripts where type overhead isn't worth it
When not using mypy/pyright (stubs have zero runtime benefit)
summary: boto3-stubs adds type annotations to boto3. pip install 'boto3-stubs[s3,dynamodb]'; from mypy_boto3_s3 import S3Client; client: S3Client = boto3.client('s3'). Full autocomplete and mypy checking for all 300+ AWS services. MIT by youtype. Zero runtime overhead — pure stubs. Install only needed service stubs. Keep version synced with boto3. Essential for any serious boto3 codebase using mypy or pyright.
